nodes.label: Investigation
nodes.description: Investigates a news article
nodes.summary: Top-level orchestrator. It kicks off scrape and news-site identification, checks ad/applicability status, resolves the article subject, alternates breadth-first owner discovery on the news-site and article-subject sides, reruns common-owner checks, ranks common owners, and persists the final result.

nodes.label: Echo
nodes.description: Echoes the input
nodes.summary: Debug callback used near startup. It verifies callback plumbing but does not affect the ownership investigation path.

nodes.label: Scrape
nodes.description: Scrapes a web page
nodes.summary: Edge-backed scraper wrapper. It returns the raw scrape payload unchanged so investigation can inspect raw HTML and extracted article text.

nodes.label: Identify News Site
nodes.description: Identifies a news site based on a url
nodes.summary: Resolves the publisher org for the article URL. It first checks the DB by domain, then falls back to search plus LLM site-name extraction, and finally persists a NewsSite-to-Entity link.

nodes.label: Search
nodes.description: Searches Brave
nodes.summary: Brave search callback wrapper. It returns raw search results for downstream cleanup and LLM interpretation.

nodes.label: Identify a company from search results
nodes.description: Takes its best guess based on the results and infobox alone, no scraping.
nodes.summary: LLM-only site-brand resolver. It uses only the provided search-result JSON to choose a public-facing brand name, tags, and evidence for the domain.

nodes.label: Check For Ad
nodes.description: Conservative detector for commerce intent, affiliate links, and whether affiliate/commission disclosure is explicit enough to trust.
nodes.summary: Runs a heuristic prepass over raw HTML and only escalates to an LLM review when the page contains meaningful commerce or disclosure signals. Investigation records its output but does not recurse through subjobs from here.

nodes.label: Check Article Applicability
nodes.description: Checks if the article is about a specific product or company.
nodes.summary: Determines whether the scraped article is specific enough to investigate. It extracts company mentions, short-circuits on zero or one result, and chooses a primary company when multiple candidates appear.

nodes.label: Extract Article Companies
nodes.description: Extracts all companies mentioned in an article.
nodes.summary: LLM extraction pass over article markdown. It returns every company mention it can ground in the body text, with prominence and short contextual role text.

nodes.label: Select Primary Company
nodes.description: Selects the most prominent company from a list of candidates.
nodes.summary: LLM tie-breaker for multi-company articles. It picks a single central company from the extracted candidate list and falls back to the first candidate when parsing fails.

nodes.label: Get Or Create Entity
nodes.description: Gets or creates an entity from Weaviate.
nodes.summary: Progressive entity resolution wrapper. It tries exact name, aliases, and fuzzy matches first, optionally validates a single fuzzy match, then runs a deeper candidate search and LLM selection before creating a new entity if nothing qualifies.

nodes.label: Compare Entities
nodes.description: Gives a confidence score as to whether or not two entities are the same.
nodes.summary: Strict LLM equivalence check used to validate a single fuzzy DB match before reusing it.

nodes.label: Get Most Likely Entity From LLM
nodes.description: Finds the most likely entity in a list given a name and context.
nodes.summary: LLM candidate selector used during deep entity search. It ranks candidate DB entities and returns the best one only if confidence exceeds a configured threshold.

nodes.label: Identify Company From Name (Multi-step)
nodes.description: Identify a company using name + optional context by gathering evidence then synthesizing a profile.
nodes.summary: Three-step company identification pipeline. Investigation uses it before owner search whenever an entity lacks `last_identification` metadata.

nodes.label: Identify Company - Gather Evidence
nodes.description: Web-search and gather evidence snippets/URLs to disambiguate a company name.
nodes.summary: Web-enabled evidence gathering pass that collects short grounded excerpts, candidate names, websites, and notes for company disambiguation.

nodes.label: Identify Company - Synthesize Identity
nodes.description: Turn gathered evidence into an identified company profile with tags, aliases, and notes.
nodes.summary: LLM synthesis pass that converts gathered evidence into a canonical/common company name, aliases, tags, notes, and a supporting evidence subset.

nodes.label: Identify Company - Categorize
nodes.description: Determines if this company is an asset manager or not.
nodes.summary: Binary classifier that separates capital/financial infrastructure companies from real-economy producers. Investigation uses its output to set the entity `top_dog` early-out behavior.

nodes.label: Find Owners - LLM
nodes.description: Find owners (and optionally investors) of a company using LLM agents.
nodes.summary: Single-entity owner discovery wrapper. It reuses cached owner relationships when available, otherwise launches consolidated parent/investor/shareholder discovery, resolves the returned owner entities, persists evidence and relationships, and hands discovered owners back to investigation for recursive traversal.

nodes.label: Find Company Parents and Investors 2
nodes.description: Find the parent companies and investors in a company.
nodes.summary: Parallel fan-out aggregator for owner discovery. It runs parent, investor, and shareholder discovery, aggregates them into a graph, dedupes equivalent names, and returns the final graph used by `find_owners_llm`.

nodes.label: Find Parent Companies
nodes.description: Finds the parent companies of a company.
nodes.summary: Web-enabled LLM that looks only for explicit owner/parent/control evidence and returns a grounded parent-company decision.

nodes.label: Find Top Investors Stacked
nodes.description: Finds the top investors in a company.
nodes.summary: Two-step investor wrapper. It first gathers investor evidence, then restructures the response into prioritized investor records.

nodes.label: Find Investors
nodes.description: Finds the investors in a company.
nodes.summary: Web-enabled LLM pass that gathers broad investor evidence for a company and returns raw JSON text for downstream structuring.

nodes.label: Structure Top Investors
nodes.description: Structure the top investors response.
nodes.summary: LLM normalization pass that converts a free-form investor response into cited high/low priority investor entries.

nodes.label: Find Top Shareholders Raw
nodes.description: It's fucking RAW!!!
nodes.summary: Shareholder-discovery wrapper. It gathers top institutional shareholders and then normalizes them into the same entity/relationship shape used by owner discovery.

nodes.label: Find Top Shareholders
nodes.description: Finds the top shareholders of a company.
nodes.summary: Web-enabled LLM pass that extracts up to the top three institutional shareholders for a company, with grounded quotes and sources.

nodes.label: Normalize Top Shareholders Results
nodes.description: Convers the results of a top shareholders search into a format matching the output from find owners.
nodes.summary: LLM schema transformer that converts raw shareholder findings into normalized `entities` and `relationships` records.

nodes.label: Aggregate Owner Results
nodes.description: Aggregates the owner/investor results into a usable shape.
nodes.summary: LLM merge pass that combines parent, investor, and shareholder outputs into one strict graph while correcting whether investor relationships belong to the target company or its parent.

nodes.label: Dedupe Entities With Web
nodes.description: Canonicalizes and deduplicates entities/relationships using web verification for identity equivalence only.
nodes.summary: Web-verified identity dedupe pass. It rewrites graph entity names to canonical forms, preserves relationships, and removes self-loops caused by merges.

nodes.label: Find Common Owners
nodes.description: Finds the common owners betwixt two companies.
nodes.summary: Database comparison job used by investigation after both sides have seed entities and after later breadth-first batches. It returns common owners plus ownership-tree context for both sides.

nodes.label: Rank Companies
nodes.description: Determines the order in which to find owners of companies.
nodes.summary: Web-enabled LLM ranking pass. Investigation uses it to rank common-owner candidates by capital influence and pick a final top owner or fallback order.

edges.condition: always near startup as an echo test
unless prefetched scrape output is supplied
unless prefetched site data is supplied
only when the site DB lookup misses or returns no linked entity
search results are cleaned, then the domain brand is inferred
always after article scrape completes
unless prefetched applicability output is supplied
always
only when more than one company is extracted
when the article is applicable, a company is identified, and no prefetched subject entity is supplied
for the news-site seed or discovered owners when the entity lacks `last_identification` metadata
only when there is exactly one fuzzy `get_entities_like` candidate and LLM validation is enabled
only during deep search when there are candidate entities to rank
always
always after evidence gathering
always after identity synthesis
for recursive owner traversal unless investigation takes the `top_dog` early-out
for the news-site seed when the entity is already identified
for the article-subject seed after entity resolution
optionally to resolve the target entity, and later once per returned owner entity in the aggregated graph
when cached owner-search metadata is not already complete
always
always
always
always
always after the raw investor response arrives
always
always after shareholder extraction
after parent, investor, and shareholder branches all finish
always after aggregate graph creation
once both seed entities exist, then again after batches and once more as a final exhaustion check when needed
only when common owners are found
investigation requeues newly discovered owner entities here if they lack identification metadata
